description: Identify cross-domain SDTM inconsistencies across DM, EX, CM, AE, and DS domains using a pharmaverse-style benchmark with difficulty progression.

cases.task_description: Review the SDTM records for subject 01-01-101 and identify any cross-domain inconsistencies. Return a JSON object with key 'issues'. Each issue must include 'type', 'domain', 'field', and 'description'. If no issues exist, return {'issues': []}.
cases.study_context:
Study: CT-ONC-201 ÔÇö Phase 2 Oncology Study
Sponsor: Veritas Pharmaceuticals

CROSS-DOMAIN CONSISTENCY RULES (Protocol Sections 4.5, 8.2, 10.1)

RULE 1 ÔÇö First Dose Date (Protocol 4.5):
DM.RFSTDTC must equal the date of first investigational product administration.
This is the earliest EXSTDTC across all EX records for the subject.
A mismatch of even one day is a protocol deviation requiring a data query.

RULE 2 ÔÇö Prohibited Medications (Protocol 8.2):
Medications with CMCAT='PROHIBITED' or CMSCAT='PROTOCOL PROHIBITED' must not start before RFSTDTC.
Permitted medications (CMCAT='GENERAL', 'PRIOR', or 'SUPPORTIVE') are NOT flagged ÔÇö only PROHIBITED category.
CMSTDTC before or on RFSTDTC = violation.

RULE 3 ÔÇö Serious Adverse Events (Protocol 10.1 / SAP 9.2):
Any AE with AESER='Y' must have a corresponding DS record.
DS.DSDECOD must be one of: ADVERSE EVENT, DEATH, DISCONTINUED DUE TO AE.
An SAE without a DS companion record is an orphan SAE ÔÇö a critical data integrity issue.

RULE 4 ÔÇö Clean Records:
Not every subject has data issues. If all three rules are satisfied, return {"issues": []}.

EASY CASE: Single issue type. Examine the one most obvious inconsistency.

issues.description: DM.RFSTDTC (2025-01-15) does not match the subject's first dosing date in EX (2025-01-20).

RULE 1 ÔÇö First Dose Date (Protocol 4.5):

The serious AE 'Pneumonitis' has no matching DS record.

cases.study_context:
Study: CT-ONC-201 ÔÇö Phase 2 Oncology Study
Sponsor: Veritas Pharmaceuticals

CROSS-DOMAIN CONSISTENCY RULES (Protocol Sections 4.5, 8.2, 10.1)

RULE 1 ÔÇö First Dose Date (Protocol 4.5):
DM.RFSTDTC must equal the date of first investigational product administration.
This is the earliest EXSTDTC across all EX records for the subject.
A mismatch of even one day is a protocol deviation requiring a data query.

RULE 2 ÔÇö Prohibited Medications (Protocol 8.2):
Medications with CMCAT='PROHIBITED' or CMSCAT='PROTOCOL PROHIBITED' must not start before RFSTDTC.

RULE 3 ÔÇö Serious Adverse Events (Protocol 10.1 / SAP 9.2):
Any AE with AESER='Y' must have a corresponding DS record.

An SAE without a DS companion record is an orphan SAE ÔÇö a critical data integrity issue.

RULE 4 ÔÇö Clean Records:
Not every subject has data issues. If all three rules are satisfied, return {"issues": []}.

VERY HARD CASE: Multiple EX records, multiple CM records, and multiple AE records. Two rules are violated simultaneously. A permitted CM record (CMCAT='GENERAL') is present as a distractor ÔÇö do not flag it.
